requestId.description: Optional caller-supplied request id for audit correlation.

timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone for the new vault. Defaults to the local system timezone.

session.description: Existing Murph assistant session id to resume.

alias.description: Optional stable alias used to map an external conversation onto one assistant session.

channel.description: Optional channel label such as telegram, linq, or email.

identity.description: Optional local assistant identity id for multi-user routing. Email routes should use the configured AgentMail inbox id.

participant.description: Optional remote participant identifier when the transport addresses a person directly. Use the transport-native participant value, such as an email correspondent; thread-addressed transports may rely on --sourceThread instead.

sourceThread.description: Optional upstream thread identifier when the transport routes by thread/chat. Use the transport-native thread value, such as a Telegram chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>` topic route; direct-recipient routes can often leave this unset.

provider.description: Chat provider adapter for the local assistant surface. The runtime is provider-backed even when only one adapter is installed.

codexCommand.description: Optional Codex CLI executable path. Defaults to `codex`.

model.description: Optional provider model override for local chat turns.

baseUrl.description: Optional OpenAI-compatible base URL for local assistant chat, such as http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 for Ollama.

apiKeyEnv.description: Optional environment variable name that stores the OpenAI-compatible API key for local assistant chat.

providerName.description: Optional stable provider label for OpenAI-compatible local assistant chat sessions.

headersJson.description: Optional flat JSON object of extra HTTP headers with string values for OpenAI-compatible local assistant chat sessions.

sandbox.description: Codex sandbox mode for local assistant chat. Codex runs as a privileged local adapter by default, so leaving this unset keeps its normal unsandboxed behavior.

approvalPolicy.description: Codex approval policy for local assistant chat. Defaults to never for the privileged local Codex adapter.

oss.description: Use Codex OSS mode, which expects a local Ollama-backed open-source provider.

deliverResponse.description: After generating a response, deliver it over the mapped outbound channel session when available.

deliveryTarget.description: Optional one-send outbound destination in the transport-native send format. For Telegram use a chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>`; for Linq use a chat id; for email use a recipient address. Reply-in-place sessions can often omit this and reuse the saved thread.

session.description: Existing Murph assistant session id to resume.

alias.description: Optional stable alias used to map an external conversation onto one assistant session.

channel.description: Optional channel label such as telegram, linq, or email.

identity.description: Optional local assistant identity id for multi-user routing. Email routes should use the configured AgentMail inbox id.

participant.description: Optional remote participant identifier when the transport addresses a person directly. Use the transport-native participant value, such as an email correspondent; thread-addressed transports may rely on --sourceThread instead.

sourceThread.description: Optional upstream thread identifier when the transport routes by thread/chat. Use the transport-native thread value, such as a Telegram chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>` topic route; direct-recipient routes can often leave this unset.

deliveryTarget.description: Optional one-send outbound destination in the transport-native send format. For Telegram use a chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>`; for Linq use a chat id; for email use a recipient address. Reply-in-place sessions can often omit this and reuse the saved thread.

requestId.description: Optional caller-supplied request id for audit correlation.

model.description: Optional model id for canonical inbox triage routing, such as gpt-oss:20b or an AI Gateway model string. Omit it when you only want channel auto-reply.

baseUrl.description: Optional OpenAI-compatible base URL for the inbox routing model.

apiKey.description: Optional explicit API key for the OpenAI-compatible routing endpoint.

apiKeyEnv.description: Optional environment variable name that stores the routing API key.

providerName.description: Optional stable provider label for the routing endpoint.

headersJson.description: Optional flat JSON object of extra HTTP headers with string values for the routing endpoint.

maxPerScan.description: Maximum inbox captures to inspect during each assistant scan.

allowSelfAuthored.description: Allow self-authored captures to trigger channel auto-reply. Useful for a dedicated assistant self-chat or sandbox thread, but only safe when you isolate that thread to Murph.

sessionRolloverHours.description: Optional maximum age for a reused assistant session in hours before Murph starts a new one for the same channel thread.

once.description: Run one assistant scan and then exit.

participant.description: Optional remote participant identifier when the transport addresses a person directly. Use the transport-native participant value, such as an email correspondent; thread-addressed transports may rely on --sourceThread instead.

sourceThread.description: Optional upstream thread identifier when the transport routes by thread/chat. Use the transport-native thread value, such as a Telegram chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>` topic route; direct-recipient routes can often leave this unset.

deliveryTarget.description: Optional saved outbound destination in the transport-native send format. For Telegram use a chat id or `<chatId>:topic:<messageThreadId>`; for Linq use a chat id; for email use a recipient address.

baseUrl.description: Override the reachable device sync control-plane URL. When omitted, Murph manages the local daemon for the selected vault. When set, Murph talks to the explicit control-plane target instead.

returnTo.description: Optional post-connect redirect. Accepts a root-relative path like /settings/devices or an absolute URL; device-syncd still rejects absolute URLs outside its allowed origin list.

open.description: Open the authorization URL in the default browser after creating the OAuth state.

section.description: Optional replacement memory section. Defaults to the current section.

show.description: Show the saved default assistant backend without changing it. When set, no update options are allowed.

preset.description: Assistant backend preset to save. Required for non-interactive updates when Murph cannot infer or reuse the backend, and required when switching between Codex and an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

providerPreset.description: Optional named OpenAI-compatible provider preset. Only applies with `--preset openai-compatible`. Seeds endpoint defaults for prompts or non-interactive saves.

model.description: Default model to save for the selected backend. In non-interactive mode, pair this with `--preset` unless Murph can reuse the currently saved backend.

baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL to save, such as http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1. Only applies with `--preset openai-compatible`.

apiKeyEnv.description: Environment variable name that should hold the OpenAI-compatible API key. Only applies with `--preset openai-compatible`.

providerName.description: Stable label for the saved OpenAI-compatible provider. Only applies with `--preset openai-compatible`.

zeroDataRetention.description: Request zero data retention on Vercel AI Gateway assistant turns. Only applies with `--preset openai-compatible`.

codexCommand.description: Optional Codex CLI executable path. Only applies with `--preset codex`. Defaults to `codex`.

profile.description: Optional Codex profile name to save. Only applies with `--preset codex`.

reasoningEffort.description: Optional assistant reasoning effort default to save for the selected backend. Use the matching `--preset` when Murph cannot infer the backend non-interactively.

oss.description: Save a local Codex OSS model target instead of the signed-in Codex cloud path. Only applies with `--preset codex`.

waypoint.description: Optional intermediate stops. Repeat --waypoint to add more than one.

profile.description: Routing profile. Use walking for hikes, runs, and on-foot trail estimates.

elevation.description: Include an approximate elevation summary from bounded terrain contour samples.

geometry.description: Include the routed GeoJSON LineString in the response.

country.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country hints for geocoding. Repeat --country to add more than one.

language.description: Optional language hint for geocoding display names.

elevationSampleSpacingMeters.description: Approximate spacing between elevation samples when --elevation is set.

maxElevationSamples.description: Maximum number of elevation sample points when --elevation is set.

body.description: Assistant-authored markdown body for the page. Do not include YAML frontmatter; Murph normalizes the heading plus generated related and sources sections.

slug.description: Optional stable page slug. Defaults to a slugified title or H1 heading.

pageType.description: Optional freeform page type such as concept, pattern, or protocol.

status.description: Optional page status such as active, draft, or archived.

clearLibraryLinks.description: Clear any existing `librarySlugs` links before applying this upsert. Use this when a prior stable reference link is stale or should be removed.

relatedSlug.description: Optional explicit related page slugs. Repeat --related-slug to add more; Murph also derives related slugs from body wikilinks.

items.description: Stable lowercase kebab-case identifier.

librarySlug.description: Optional stable `bank/library` entity slugs that this personal wiki page builds on. Repeat --library-slug to include multiple reference entities.

items.description: Stable lowercase kebab-case identifier.

sourcePath.description: Optional vault-relative source file paths, or absolute source file paths that still resolve inside the selected vault. Repeat --source-path to include multiple files. Derived/runtime paths such as derived/** and .runtime/** are rejected.

items.description: Filesystem path supplied by the operator.

account.description: Optional account identity for the connector. Defaults to `bot` for Telegram, `default` for Linq, and should be an AgentMail inbox id for email unless `--provision` is used; for AgentMail this is often the inbox id or inbox email address.

address.description: Optional email address to associate with an existing AgentMail inbox connector.

backfillLimit.description: Default backfill limit for this connector.

provision.description: Attempt to provision a new AgentMail inbox when adding an email connector. If create permission is unavailable but the API key can access exactly one existing inbox, Murph reuses that inbox automatically.

emailDisplayName.description: Optional display name to use when provisioning a new AgentMail inbox.

emailUsername.description: Optional mailbox username when provisioning a new AgentMail inbox.

emailDomain.description: Optional AgentMail domain when provisioning a new AgentMail inbox.

emailClientId.description: Optional AgentMail client id to associate when provisioning a new inbox.

linqWebhookHost.description: Optional local bind host for the Linq webhook listener. Defaults to 0.0.0.0.

linqWebhookPath.description: Optional local path for the Linq webhook listener. Defaults to /linq-webhook.

linqWebhookPort.description: Optional local port for the Linq webhook listener. Defaults to 8789.

enableAutoReply.description: Enable assistant auto-reply for this connector channel after the source is added.
